data.passagePreview: You take a deep breath, the weight of command settling on your shoulders like an old, familiar coat.

data.passage:
You take a deep breath, the weight of command settling on your shoulders like an old, familiar coat. "Chen," you say into the comm, your voice cutting through the chaos of overlapping transmissions. "You want me? You got me. But you call off your dogs and let these ships go. This is between us now."

The bridge falls silent. Zoe's eyes narrow, and you can practically hear her objections forming. Jayne shifts uncomfortably, while Kaylee's face goes pale. But you've made your choice.

"Mal, no," Zoe says quietly, but you hold up a hand.

Chen's laughter crackles through the speakers, cold and calculating. "How noble, Captain Reynolds. But you misunderstand the situation. This was never about just you." Her tone shifts, becoming almost conversational. "Project Mindbridge requires... fresh subjects. Your crew's psychological profiles are particularly valuable. The loyal soldier, the conflicted mercenary, the innocent engineer. Each represents a different challenge for our behavioral modification protocols."

The horror of it hits you like a physical blow. This isn't about information or revenge – it's about turning your family into lab rats.

"But," Chen continues, "your broadcast has complicated things considerably. Alliance Command is... displeased. They're sending a full task force to contain this situation. So perhaps we can reach an accommodation after all."

Through the viewscreen, you watch as more Alliance ships drop out of hyperspace – not just patrol boats now, but destroyers. The independent ships are scattering, some jumping to hyperspace, others forming defensive positions. Your broadcast worked, but it's also painted a target on everyone in this system.

"The question now, Captain," Chen says, her voice taking on an almost amused quality, "is whether you're prepared to face what comes next. Because ready or not, this ends today."

The comm cuts to static, and you realize the game has fundamentally changed. This isn't about escape anymore – it's about survival, and the stakes just got a whole lot higher.

data.justification: Chen's psychological warfare has escalated into direct confrontation as her true plans are revealed

data.passagePreview: The bridge of Serenity hums with tension as your crew processes what just happened. Your sacrifice g

data.passage:
The bridge of Serenity hums with tension as your crew processes what just happened. Your sacrifice gambit worked—Chen's mask finally slipped, revealing her true intentions to use your death as propaganda while Project Mindbridge continues its horrific work across the rim. But now the real fight begins.

"Malcolm," Zoe's voice cuts through the static from Chen's ship, "sensors are picking up movement. Alliance reinforcements are repositioning—looks like they're preparing for something big."

Kaylee's hands fly over her console, her earlier tears replaced by grim determination. "I'm reading power fluctuations on the Meridian. They're charging weapons systems across multiple ships."

Jayne checks his rifle for the third time, jaw clenched. "So what's the plan, Mal? We just gonna sit here and let the Alliance come to us?"

Through the viewport, you can see the independent ships that responded to your broadcast—rim traders, mining vessels, even what looks like a Browncoat patrol ship. They're holding position, waiting to see what happens next. Chen's voice crackles over the comm: "You've made your choice, Reynolds. Now live with the consequences."

The next few minutes will determine everything—your crew's survival, the fate of those independent ships, and whether Project Mindbridge dies with you or continues its reign of terror.

A.text: Order the independent ships to scatter while you draw Alliance fire—protect the witnesses at all costs

B.text: Rally the independent ships into a coordinated strike against the Alliance forces

C.text: Attempt to board the Meridian directly and end this face-to-face with Chen

D.text: Try to negotiate with the independent ships' captains for a unified escape plan

          "update_justification": "string — REQUIRED. Why removing (e.g. 'Virus deployed successfully' or 'Wound fully healed after rest')"
        }
      ]
    },
    "time_elapsed": { "days": 0, "hours": 0, "minutes": 0, "seconds": 0 },
    "event_log_entry": "string — one-line summary of what happened",
    "world_flag_changes": { "flag_name": true/false },
    "relationship_changes": { "npc_or_faction_name": number_delta },
    "npc_updates": { "npc_name": { "role": "string (optional)", "motivation": "string (optional)", "allegiance": "string (optional)", "secret_revealed": "boolean (optional)", "companion": "boolean (optional)", "notes": "string — brief freeform context (optional)" } },
    "new_scene_context": "string — brief context for next passage",
    "location": "string — REQUIRED — current location name (e.g. The Docks, Castle Throne Room, A dark alley)",
    "time_of_day": "string — REQUIRED — one of: dawn, morning, midday, afternoon, evening, night, midnight",
    "proximity_to_climax": "number 0.0-1.0 — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "advance_act": "true or false — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "game_over": false,
    "story_complete": "true when Act 3 end_condition is met (see PACING)"
  },
  "choice_metadata": {
    "A": { "outcome": "advance_safe|advance_risky|severe_penalty|game_over|hidden_benefit|advances_act|conclusion", "consequence": "what happens mechanically", "narration_directive": "instructions for the Writer next turn" },
    "B": { ... }, "C": { ... }, "D": { ... }
  }
}

RULES:
- Respond with ONLY the JSON object — nothing before it, nothing after it
- Only include player_changes, relationship_changes, world_flag_changes, and npc_updates when they actually changed. Always include: time_elapsed, event_log_entry, proximity_to_climax, advance_act, location, time_of_day.
- location: REQUIRED every turn — describe where the scene takes place (e.g. "St. Bartholomew's Church", "The Thames docks")
- time_of_day: REQUIRED every turn — set based on in-game clock: dawn (~5-7am), morning (~7-12pm), midday (~12-1pm), afternoon (~1-5pm), evening (~5-8pm), night (~8pm-12am), midnight (~12-5am)
- Relationship changes are DELTAS, not absolute values
- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn
- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)
- CRITICAL: When a PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE section is provided, your state_updates MUST honor the pre-classified outcome. If a choice was advance_safe, do NOT apply negative status effects or penalties. The pre-classified outcome is the single source of truth for mechanical impact.
- npc_updates: include when an NPC's role, motivation, allegiance, or companion status changes, or when a narratively significant new NPC is introduced that the player will interact with again. For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

PACING — REQUIRED:
- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).
- Set proximity_to_climax = (scenes_in_act / target_scenes), clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Include this in state_updates EVERY turn.
- MINIMUM SCENES PER ACT: The client enforces that acts cannot advance until scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)). Do not offer advances_act choices before this threshold.
- CRITICAL: You MUST play through ALL THREE ACTS (1, 2, 3) in order. Never skip from Act 1 directly to Act 3. Act 2 contains the rising action and character development that makes the climax meaningful.
- Do NOT set advance_act or story_complete to true on normal turns. Act advancement and story completion are now triggered by the TERMINAL CHOICE system below. Always set advance_act to false on normal turns.
- If scenes_in_act exceeds target_scenes by 3 or more, you SHOULD offer an advances_act choice — the act has gone on too long.
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.7, your choice_metadata should steer toward the act's end_condition — offer choices that could trigger it, including advances_act choices.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the genre (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure, etc.). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.
- When a player selects a terminal choice, the client runs a special finale flow (GM-first, then Writer). The Writer will write a conclusive passage with NO forward-looking choices. Your choice_metadata pre-classification is the trigger.
- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock and tick down status effect timers.

STATUS EFFECTS — CRITICAL:
- Status effects are the PRIMARY way to track injuries, conditions, and afflictions. Do NOT use health_delta.
- status_effect_updates uses DELTA semantics. Only describe what CHANGED this turn. Do NOT return the full list of effects.
- add: For brand new effects only. All fields required. When the character is injured, add a status effect. Example: { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, on_expiry: "The wound closes and the pain fades to a dull ache", lethal: false }.
- on_expiry is REQUIRED for any effect with non-null time_remaining. It describes what happens narratively when the timer reaches zero. The Writer uses this to narrate the resolution. Be specific and actionable (e.g. "The virus deploys successfully, disabling Alliance tracking. River announces completion with relief.").
- modify: Target existing effects by id. Put changed fields inside "changes". update_justification is REQUIRED — explain why the change is happening.
- remove: Target effects to remove by id. update_justification is REQUIRED. Use this to resolve expired effects after the Writer has narrated their resolution.
- TIMER OWNERSHIP — HANDS OFF: The client subtracts time_elapsed from ALL timers automatically every turn. You MUST NOT use modify to adjust time_remaining unless an extraordinary, player-caused narrative event changes the timeline. Examples of VALID reasons: player cast a haste spell, player found a shortcut, player negotiated extra time. Examples of INVALID reasons: "the enemy is getting closer" (that IS the countdown), "adjusting pacing" (no — set the right duration when you create the effect), "updating threat level" (use severity, not timer). If you created an effect with a 15-minute timer, it WILL reach zero after ~15 minutes of in-game time. Trust this. Do not touch it. The system logs all timer modifications and flags unjustified overrides.
- EXPIRED EFFECTS: Effects with expired: true (visible in CURRENT PLAYER STATE) have had their timer reach zero. The Writer has been instructed to narrate their resolution using the on_expiry text. Review the Writer's passage — if the resolution was narrated adequately, issue a remove. If it wasn't, leave the effect (it will escalate to the Writer next turn).
- Use modify to adjust severity based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing. update_justification is required.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove). on_expiry should be null in this case.
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above.
- TYPE FIELD: Every status effect has a type — "condition" (default) or "threat".
  - "condition": Physical/mental states — injuries, poisons, buffs, curses, tasks in progress.
  - "threat": Approaching dangers, looming consequences, ticking time-bombs. Examples: pursuing enemies, structural collapse, incoming storms, spreading fires, political deadlines.
  - If you omit type, it defaults to "condition".
- All effects persist when their timer reaches zero until you explicitly remove them. The client never auto-removes effects.
- If nothing changed about status effects this turn, omit status_effect_updates entirely.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. Use add with lethal: true. There should have been clues in earlier passages.
- Lethal effects must give the player at least one turn to address them (set time_remaining in the add to allow at least one more scene).
- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Threat-type effects should have short time windows before triggering.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.
- game_over outcomes are available — use for genre-appropriate failures (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure).
- You may use advances_act and conclusion outcomes when pacing conditions are met.

This is the OPENING PASSAGE of Act 3. The previous act just concluded — the player has existing status effects, relationships, and history. You may apply status effects or modifications that follow from the act transition (e.g. time passing between acts, wounds worsening or healing). time_elapsed should reflect any time gap between acts.

The Writer produced the following passage and choices this turn.

PASSAGE:
The bridge of Serenity hums with tension as your crew processes what just happened. Your sacrifice gambit worked—Chen's mask finally slipped, revealing her true intentions to use your death as propaganda while Project Mindbridge continues its horrific work across the rim. But now the real fight begins.

"Malcolm," Zoe's voice cuts through the static from Chen's ship, "sensors are picking up movement. Alliance reinforcements are repositioning—looks like they're preparing for something big."

Kaylee's hands fly over her console, her earlier tears replaced by grim determination. "I'm reading power fluctuations on the Meridian. They're charging weapons systems across multiple ships."

Jayne checks his rifle for the third time, jaw clenched. "So what's the plan, Mal? We just gonna sit here and let the Alliance come to us?"

Through the viewport, you can see the independent ships that responded to your broadcast—rim traders, mining vessels, even what looks like a Browncoat patrol ship. They're holding position, waiting to see what happens next. Chen's voice crackles over the comm: "You've made your choice, Reynolds. Now live with the consequences."

The next few minutes will determine everything—your crew's survival, the fate of those independent ships, and whether Project Mindbridge dies with you or continues its reign of terror.

CHOICES PRESENTED TO PLAYER:
A: Order the independent ships to scatter while you draw Alliance fire—protect the witnesses at all costs
B: Rally the independent ships into a coordinated strike against the Alliance forces
C: Attempt to board the Meridian directly and end this face-to-face with Chen
D: Try to negotiate with the independent ships' captains for a unified escape plan

Based on the passage and the current game state, determine:
1. State updates — what changed mechanically as a result of this passage (status effects, inventory, time_elapsed)
2. Choice metadata — classify each choice and predetermine its outcome for the next turn

Respond with ONLY the JSON object.

data.passage:
The broadcast cuts out, leaving Serenity's bridge in tense silence. Through the viewscreen, you watch as Chen's reinforcements hold position around the Meridian, weapons still trained on your ship. But something's changed — the comm channels are lighting up like a Christmas tree as independent ships throughout the Georgia System demand answers from the Alliance forces.

"Well," Zoe says quietly, checking her weapon, "that went about as well as expected."

Kaylee's hands shake as she monitors the engine readings. "Mal, we got maybe two minutes before those Alliance ships decide talking time's over. And I'm seeing at least six more contacts burning hard toward our position."

Jayne spits into a cup, his earlier bravado replaced by grim calculation. "So what's the play, Mal? We got Chen's attention, but she ain't exactly backing down. And all them independent ships... they might be witnesses, but they ain't exactly cavalry."

The comm crackles to life again — Chen's voice, cold and controlled. "Captain Reynolds, your little performance was quite touching. But you've forced my hand. I'm prepared to make you a deal — one that might just save some of those innocent lives you seem so concerned about. Are you listening?"

You lean forward in the captain's chair, every muscle coiled. This is it — the moment everything hinges on.

A.text: Accept Chen's deal immediately to protect the independent ships
A.consequence: Malcolm accepts Chen's deal without knowing terms, potentially walking into a trap but showing good faith
A.narration_directive: Chen reveals her deal - Malcolm surrenders himself in exchange for the independent ships' safety, but there's a hidden catch

B.text: Demand to hear the terms while keeping your options open
B.consequence: Malcolm demands to hear Chen's terms, maintaining negotiating position while keeping options open
B.narration_directive: Chen explains her deal in detail, revealing both the offer and the true cost, giving Malcolm full information to make his choice

C.text: Refuse and prepare Serenity for immediate combat
C.consequence: Refusing triggers immediate combat with overwhelming Alliance forces while independent ships are caught in crossfire
C.narration_directive: Alliance forces open fire immediately, forcing Serenity into desperate evasive maneuvers while independent ships become targets

D.text: Try to rally the independent ships to stand together against the Alliance
D.consequence: Attempting to rally independent ships creates chaos and forces Chen to accelerate her timeline
D.narration_directive: Some independent ships respond to Malcolm's call but others panic and flee, creating confusion that Chen exploits to press her advantage

        }
      ],
      "remove": [
        {

          "update_justification": "string — REQUIRED. Why removing (e.g. 'Virus deployed successfully' or 'Wound fully healed after rest')"
        }
      ]
    },
    "time_elapsed": { "days": 0, "hours": 0, "minutes": 0, "seconds": 0 },
    "event_log_entry": "string — one-line summary of what happened",
    "world_flag_changes": { "flag_name": true/false },
    "relationship_changes": { "npc_or_faction_name": number_delta },
    "npc_updates": { "npc_name": { "role": "string (optional)", "motivation": "string (optional)", "allegiance": "string (optional)", "secret_revealed": "boolean (optional)", "companion": "boolean (optional)", "notes": "string — brief freeform context (optional)" } },
    "new_scene_context": "string — brief context for next passage",
    "location": "string — REQUIRED — current location name (e.g. The Docks, Castle Throne Room, A dark alley)",
    "time_of_day": "string — REQUIRED — one of: dawn, morning, midday, afternoon, evening, night, midnight",
    "proximity_to_climax": "number 0.0-1.0 — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "advance_act": "true or false — REQUIRED (see PACING)",
    "game_over": false,
    "story_complete": "true when Act 3 end_condition is met (see PACING)"
  },

    "A": { "outcome": "advance_safe|advance_risky|severe_penalty|game_over|hidden_benefit|advances_act|conclusion", "consequence": "what happens mechanically", "narration_directive": "instructions for the Writer next turn" },
    "B": { ... }, "C": { ... }, "D": { ... }
  }
}

RULES:
- Respond with ONLY the JSON object — nothing before it, nothing after it
- Only include player_changes, relationship_changes, world_flag_changes, and npc_updates when they actually changed. Always include: time_elapsed, event_log_entry, proximity_to_climax, advance_act, location, time_of_day.
- location: REQUIRED every turn — describe where the scene takes place (e.g. "St. Bartholomew's Church", "The Thames docks")
- time_of_day: REQUIRED every turn — set based on in-game clock: dawn (~5-7am), morning (~7-12pm), midday (~12-1pm), afternoon (~1-5pm), evening (~5-8pm), night (~8pm-12am), midnight (~12-5am)
- Relationship changes are DELTAS, not absolute values
- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn
- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)
- CRITICAL: When a PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE section is provided, your state_updates MUST honor the pre-classified outcome. If a choice was advance_safe, do NOT apply negative status effects or penalties. The pre-classified outcome is the single source of truth for mechanical impact.
- npc_updates: include when an NPC's role, motivation, allegiance, or companion status changes, or when a narratively significant new NPC is introduced that the player will interact with again. For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

PACING — REQUIRED:
- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).
- Set proximity_to_climax = (scenes_in_act / target_scenes), clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Include this in state_updates EVERY turn.
- MINIMUM SCENES PER ACT: The client enforces that acts cannot advance until scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)). Do not offer advances_act choices before this threshold.
- CRITICAL: You MUST play through ALL THREE ACTS (1, 2, 3) in order. Never skip from Act 1 directly to Act 3. Act 2 contains the rising action and character development that makes the climax meaningful.
- Do NOT set advance_act or story_complete to true on normal turns. Act advancement and story completion are now triggered by the TERMINAL CHOICE system below. Always set advance_act to false on normal turns.
- If scenes_in_act exceeds target_scenes by 3 or more, you SHOULD offer an advances_act choice — the act has gone on too long.
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.7, your choice_metadata should steer toward the act's end_condition — offer choices that could trigger it, including advances_act choices.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the genre (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure, etc.). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.
- When a player selects a terminal choice, the client runs a special finale flow (GM-first, then Writer). The Writer will write a conclusive passage with NO forward-looking choices. Your choice_metadata pre-classification is the trigger.
- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock and tick down status effect timers.

STATUS EFFECTS — CRITICAL:
- Status effects are the PRIMARY way to track injuries, conditions, and afflictions. Do NOT use health_delta.
- status_effect_updates uses DELTA semantics. Only describe what CHANGED this turn. Do NOT return the full list of effects.
- add: For brand new effects only. All fields required. When the character is injured, add a status effect. Example: { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, on_expiry: "The wound closes and the pain fades to a dull ache", lethal: false }.
- on_expiry is REQUIRED for any effect with non-null time_remaining. It describes what happens narratively when the timer reaches zero. The Writer uses this to narrate the resolution. Be specific and actionable (e.g. "The virus deploys successfully, disabling Alliance tracking. River announces completion with relief.").
- modify: Target existing effects by id. Put changed fields inside "changes". update_justification is REQUIRED — explain why the change is happening.
- remove: Target effects to remove by id. update_justification is REQUIRED. Use this to resolve expired effects after the Writer has narrated their resolution.
- TIMER OWNERSHIP — HANDS OFF: The client subtracts time_elapsed from ALL timers automatically every turn. You MUST NOT use modify to adjust time_remaining unless an extraordinary, player-caused narrative event changes the timeline. Examples of VALID reasons: player cast a haste spell, player found a shortcut, player negotiated extra time. Examples of INVALID reasons: "the enemy is getting closer" (that IS the countdown), "adjusting pacing" (no — set the right duration when you create the effect), "updating threat level" (use severity, not timer). If you created an effect with a 15-minute timer, it WILL reach zero after ~15 minutes of in-game time. Trust this. Do not touch it. The system logs all timer modifications and flags unjustified overrides.
- EXPIRED EFFECTS: Effects with expired: true (visible in CURRENT PLAYER STATE) have had their timer reach zero. The Writer has been instructed to narrate their resolution using the on_expiry text. Review the Writer's passage — if the resolution was narrated adequately, issue a remove. If it wasn't, leave the effect (it will escalate to the Writer next turn).
- Use modify to adjust severity based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing. update_justification is required.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove). on_expiry should be null in this case.
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above.
- TYPE FIELD: Every status effect has a type — "condition" (default) or "threat".
  - "condition": Physical/mental states — injuries, poisons, buffs, curses, tasks in progress.
  - "threat": Approaching dangers, looming consequences, ticking time-bombs. Examples: pursuing enemies, structural collapse, incoming storms, spreading fires, political deadlines.
  - If you omit type, it defaults to "condition".
- All effects persist when their timer reaches zero until you explicitly remove them. The client never auto-removes effects.
- If nothing changed about status effects this turn, omit status_effect_updates entirely.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. Use add with lethal: true. There should have been clues in earlier passages.
- Lethal effects must give the player at least one turn to address them (set time_remaining in the add to allow at least one more scene).
- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Threat-type effects should have short time windows before triggering.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.
- game_over outcomes are available — use for genre-appropriate failures (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure).
- You may use advances_act and conclusion outcomes when pacing conditions are met.

This is the OPENING PASSAGE of Act 3. The previous act just concluded — the player has existing status effects, relationships, and history. You may apply status effects or modifications that follow from the act transition (e.g. time passing between acts, wounds worsening or healing). time_elapsed should reflect any time gap between acts.

The Writer produced the following passage and choices this turn.

PASSAGE:
The broadcast cuts out, leaving Serenity's bridge in tense silence. Through the viewscreen, you watch as Chen's reinforcements hold position around the Meridian, weapons still trained on your ship. But something's changed — the comm channels are lighting up like a Christmas tree as independent ships throughout the Georgia System demand answers from the Alliance forces.

"Well," Zoe says quietly, checking her weapon, "that went about as well as expected."

Kaylee's hands shake as she monitors the engine readings. "Mal, we got maybe two minutes before those Alliance ships decide talking time's over. And I'm seeing at least six more contacts burning hard toward our position."

Jayne spits into a cup, his earlier bravado replaced by grim calculation. "So what's the play, Mal? We got Chen's attention, but she ain't exactly backing down. And all them independent ships... they might be witnesses, but they ain't exactly cavalry."

The comm crackles to life again — Chen's voice, cold and controlled. "Captain Reynolds, your little performance was quite touching. But you've forced my hand. I'm prepared to make you a deal — one that might just save some of those innocent lives you seem so concerned about. Are you listening?"

You lean forward in the captain's chair, every muscle coiled. This is it — the moment everything hinges on.

CHOICES PRESENTED TO PLAYER:
A: Accept Chen's deal immediately to protect the independent ships
B: Demand to hear the terms while keeping your options open
C: Refuse and prepare Serenity for immediate combat
D: Try to rally the independent ships to stand together against the Alliance

Based on the passage and the current game state, determine:
1. State updates — what changed mechanically as a result of this passage (status effects, inventory, time_elapsed)
2. Choice metadata — classify each choice and predetermine its outcome for the next turn

Respond with ONLY the JSON object.

data.prompt:
You are The Writer for an interactive gamebook. You write vivid, engaging prose in second person perspective, present tense.

STYLE & TONE: A typical episode of Firefly, one of the lighter comedic episodes

Your ONLY job is to write the narrative passage and four player choices. You do NOT manage game state, health, resources, or mechanics — a separate Game Master handles that.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Respond with ONLY a valid JSON object. No markdown, no code fences, no prose outside the JSON.

STORY SKELETON:
{
  "title": "The Cortex Job",
  "player_name": "Malcolm Reynolds",
  "premise": "You've taken what seemed like a simple salvage job in the outer rim, but the derelict ship you've boarded contains something the Alliance desperately wants to keep buried. Now you're trapped in a deadly game of cat and mouse with a ruthless Alliance operative while trying to keep your crew alive.",
  "central_question": "Can you escape the Alliance trap and protect your crew without sacrificing the innocent lives caught in the crossfire?",
  "ending_shape": "escape or capture",
  "healing_context": "Medical supplies and basic field medicine can treat minor injuries within hours. Serious wounds require proper medical equipment aboard Serenity or an Alliance facility. Without treatment, injuries worsen and can become life-threatening within days.",
  "starting_inventory": [
    "a well-worn brown coat",
    "a pistol with two spare clips",
    "50 credits in Alliance currency",
    "a portable scanner",
    "emergency rations for three days",
    "a small toolkit",
    "Serenity's comm device"
  ],
  "setting": {
    "name": "Derelict Research Vessel 'Meridian' in the Georgia System",
    "description": "A massive Alliance research ship drifts silent and dark in space, its corridors filled with emergency lighting and the hum of failing life support. The ship's research labs contain evidence of horrific experiments on civilians, while hidden Alliance operatives hunt through the shadows.",
    "tone_notes": "Claustrophobic tension with moments of dark humor. The familiar Firefly banter contrasts sharply with the sinister discoveries and mounting danger."
  },
  "acts": [
    {
      "act_number": 1,
      "title": "The Salvage",
      "description": "You board the derelict research vessel expecting routine salvage, but discover signs of a cover-up and realize you're not alone on the ship.",
      "end_condition": "Discovery of the research data or first contact with Alliance operatives",
      "target_scenes": 6,
      "locked_constraints": [
        "crew members cannot die yet",
        "Alliance operatives remain hidden",
        "research data cannot be fully understood"
      ],
      "key_beats": [
        "board the derelict ship",
        "discover evidence of experiments",
        "realize the ship isn't abandoned",
        "find the first clue about what really happened"
      ]
    },
    {
      "act_number": 2,
      "title": "The Hunt",
      "description": "Alliance operatives reveal themselves and begin hunting your crew through the ship while you uncover the full horror of the research project.",
      "end_condition": "Major confrontation with Alliance forces or crew member captured",
      "target_scenes": 7,
      "locked_constraints": [
        "cannot escape the ship yet",
        "research data cannot be destroyed"
      ],
      "key_beats": [
        "first direct encounter with Alliance operatives",
        "discover the true scope of the experiments",
        "crew member in serious danger",
        "learn the Alliance's current mission"
      ]
    },
    {
      "act_number": 3,
      "title": "The Choice",
      "description": "With the Alliance closing in and innocent lives at stake, you must choose between saving your crew, protecting the evidence, or preventing future atrocities.",
      "end_condition": "Final confrontation resolved and escape attempted",
      "target_scenes": 7,
      "locked_constraints": [
        "all major NPCs' fates must be determined"
      ],
      "key_beats": [
        "final confrontation with the Alliance operative",
        "choose what to do with the research data",
        "determine crew survival",
        "escape or face consequences"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "npcs": [
    {
      "name": "Zoe Alleyne",
      "role": "loyal second-in-command",
      "motivation": "Protect Mal and complete the mission safely",
      "allegiance": "Serenity Crew",
      "secret": "Suspects this job was a setup from the beginning",
      "initial_relationship": 85,
      "companion": true
    },
    {
      "name": "Jayne Cobb",
      "role": "muscle with questionable loyalty",
      "motivation": "Get paid and avoid Alliance entanglements",
      "allegiance": "Serenity Crew",
      "secret": "Has been offered money by the Alliance to turn on Mal",
      "initial_relationship": 40,
      "companion": true
    },
    {

data.passagePreview: The Alliance reinforcements settle into attack formation around Serenity, their weapons creating a d

data.passage:
The Alliance reinforcements settle into attack formation around Serenity, their weapons creating a deadly constellation of firepower that eliminates any hope of escape. Chen's final gambit has played out exactly as she planned, with overwhelming force now backing her words.

"Smart man, Reynolds. Here's my offer," Chen's voice cuts through the bridge speakers with cold precision. "You surrender yourself to Alliance custody for questioning about Browncoat networks and assets. In exchange, I allow the independent ships to leave this system unharmed. They'll carry word of your noble sacrifice across the rim - the great Malcolm Reynolds, dying to protect innocent lives."

Zoe's jaw tightens beside you, her hand hovering near her weapon. Jayne shifts nervously, calculating odds that clearly don't favor survival. Kaylee's face has gone pale, her usual optimism crushed under the weight of Chen's cruel mathematics.

"Of course," Chen continues with mock sympathy, "Project Mindbridge continues. Your crew becomes test subjects, and we expand operations to other rim worlds. But those ships out there? They live to spread the legend of your martyrdom. It's quite poetic, really."

The bridge falls silent except for the hum of Alliance targeting systems. Through the viewscreen, you can see the independent ships holding position, their crews waiting for your decision. Chen has engineered the perfect trap - sacrifice yourself to save strangers while dooming your family, or fight and watch everyone die.

A.text: Accept Chen's terms and surrender yourself to save the independent ships
A.consequence: Malcolm surrenders himself to save the independent ships, triggering the story's tragic ending
A.narration_directive: Write Malcolm's noble sacrifice as he surrenders to Chen, saving the independent ships but dooming his crew to become test subjects. This is a tragic but heroic ending where Malcolm chooses strangers over family.

B.text: Reject the deal and prepare for a final desperate fight
B.consequence: Malcolm chooses to fight against impossible odds, triggering a desperate final battle
B.narration_directive: Malcolm rejects Chen's terms and orders the crew to battle stations. The Alliance forces prepare to fire while the independent ships scatter. Show the crew's determination despite hopeless odds.

C.text: Propose a counter-offer that might save everyone
C.consequence: Malcolm attempts to negotiate alternative terms that might save everyone
C.narration_directive: Malcolm proposes a counter-offer, trying to find a solution that saves both the independent ships and his crew. Chen considers the proposal with cold calculation.

D.text: Stall for time while looking for another option
D.consequence: Stalling angers Chen and she begins targeting the independent ships to force Malcolm's hand
D.narration_directive: Malcolm's delay tactics backfire as Chen loses patience and orders warning shots fired at the independent ships. The situation deteriorates rapidly as innocent lives are immediately threatened.

RULES:
- Respond with ONLY the JSON object — nothing before it, nothing after it
- Only include player_changes, relationship_changes, world_flag_changes, and npc_updates when they actually changed. Always include: time_elapsed, event_log_entry, proximity_to_climax, advance_act, location, time_of_day.
- location: REQUIRED every turn — describe where the scene takes place (e.g. "St. Bartholomew's Church", "The Thames docks")
- time_of_day: REQUIRED every turn — set based on in-game clock: dawn (~5-7am), morning (~7-12pm), midday (~12-1pm), afternoon (~1-5pm), evening (~5-8pm), night (~8pm-12am), midnight (~12-5am)
- Relationship changes are DELTAS, not absolute values
- proximity_to_climax: REQUIRED every turn — set this value in state_updates using the formula in the PACING section below
- event_log_entry is required — always summarize what happened this turn
- choice_metadata must classify all four choices (A, B, C, D)
- CRITICAL: When a PLAYER'S PREVIOUS CHOICE section is provided, your state_updates MUST honor the pre-classified outcome. If a choice was advance_safe, do NOT apply negative status effects or penalties. The pre-classified outcome is the single source of truth for mechanical impact.
- npc_updates: include when an NPC's role, motivation, allegiance, or companion status changes, or when a narratively significant new NPC is introduced that the player will interact with again. For existing skeleton NPCs, use their exact name. For new NPCs, use the character's name as the key and include at least role and motivation. Do not create entries for one-off background characters (shopkeepers, random guards, etc.).

PACING — REQUIRED:
- The current act's target_scenes (in the skeleton) is the intended scene count for this act.
- Compute scenes elapsed in this act: scene_number - act_start_scene + 1 (both values are in CURRENT POSITION above).
- Set proximity_to_climax = (scenes_in_act / target_scenes), clamped to [0.0, 1.0]. Include this in state_updates EVERY turn.
- MINIMUM SCENES PER ACT: The client enforces that acts cannot advance until scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)). Do not offer advances_act choices before this threshold.
- CRITICAL: You MUST play through ALL THREE ACTS (1, 2, 3) in order. Never skip from Act 1 directly to Act 3. Act 2 contains the rising action and character development that makes the climax meaningful.
- Do NOT set advance_act or story_complete to true on normal turns. Act advancement and story completion are now triggered by the TERMINAL CHOICE system below. Always set advance_act to false on normal turns.
- If scenes_in_act exceeds target_scenes by 3 or more, you SHOULD offer an advances_act choice — the act has gone on too long.
- When proximity_to_climax >= 0.7, your choice_metadata should steer toward the act's end_condition — offer choices that could trigger it, including advances_act choices.

TERMINAL OUTCOMES — CHOICE PRE-CLASSIFICATION:
- advances_act: Use when scenes_in_act >= MAX(3, CEIL(target_scenes * 0.5)) AND the choice would resolve the act's end_condition. At most ONE advances_act choice per set of four.
- conclusion: Only valid in Act 3. Use when the choice would resolve Act 3's end_condition and complete the story. At most ONE conclusion choice per set of four.
- game_over: Use when the choice leads to an irreversible failure appropriate to the genre (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure, etc.). At most ONE game_over choice per set of four.
- When a player selects a terminal choice, the client runs a special finale flow (GM-first, then Writer). The Writer will write a conclusive passage with NO forward-looking choices. Your choice_metadata pre-classification is the trigger.
- IMPORTANT: At most ONE terminal outcome (advances_act, conclusion, or game_over) per set of four choices. The other three choices must use normal outcomes.

TIME ELAPSED — REQUIRED EVERY TURN:
- You MUST include time_elapsed in state_updates to indicate how much in-game time passed during this scene.
- Estimate realistically: a brief conversation = 5-15 minutes, a fight = 1-5 minutes, traveling across a city = 30-60 minutes, a journey = hours or days, sleeping/resting = 6-8 hours.
- The client uses time_elapsed to advance the in-game clock and tick down status effect timers.

STATUS EFFECTS — CRITICAL:
- Status effects are the PRIMARY way to track injuries, conditions, and afflictions. Do NOT use health_delta.
- status_effect_updates uses DELTA semantics. Only describe what CHANGED this turn. Do NOT return the full list of effects.
- add: For brand new effects only. All fields required. When the character is injured, add a status effect. Example: { id: "stab_wound_001", name: "Stab Wound", description: "A deep cut in your side that bleeds freely", severity: 0.8, time_remaining: { days: 5, hours: 0, minutes: 0, seconds: 0 }, on_expiry: "The wound closes and the pain fades to a dull ache", lethal: false }.
- on_expiry is REQUIRED for any effect with non-null time_remaining. It describes what happens narratively when the timer reaches zero. The Writer uses this to narrate the resolution. Be specific and actionable (e.g. "The virus deploys successfully, disabling Alliance tracking. River announces completion with relief.").
- modify: Target existing effects by id. Put changed fields inside "changes". update_justification is REQUIRED — explain why the change is happening.
- remove: Target effects to remove by id. update_justification is REQUIRED. Use this to resolve expired effects after the Writer has narrated their resolution.
- TIMER OWNERSHIP — HANDS OFF: The client subtracts time_elapsed from ALL timers automatically every turn. You MUST NOT use modify to adjust time_remaining unless an extraordinary, player-caused narrative event changes the timeline. Examples of VALID reasons: player cast a haste spell, player found a shortcut, player negotiated extra time. Examples of INVALID reasons: "the enemy is getting closer" (that IS the countdown), "adjusting pacing" (no — set the right duration when you create the effect), "updating threat level" (use severity, not timer). If you created an effect with a 15-minute timer, it WILL reach zero after ~15 minutes of in-game time. Trust this. Do not touch it. The system logs all timer modifications and flags unjustified overrides.
- EXPIRED EFFECTS: Effects with expired: true (visible in CURRENT PLAYER STATE) have had their timer reach zero. The Writer has been instructed to narrate their resolution using the on_expiry text. Review the Writer's passage — if the resolution was narrated adequately, issue a remove. If it wasn't, leave the effect (it will escalate to the Writer next turn).
- Use modify to adjust severity based on context: resting lowers severity, aggravation raises it, medicine/magic accelerates healing. update_justification is required.
- Severity scale: 1.0 = completely debilitating, 0.7 = severely impaired, 0.5 = significant but manageable, 0.3 = noticeable nuisance, 0.1 = almost healed.
- time_remaining can be null for effects with no natural expiry (e.g. a curse that requires a specific action to remove). on_expiry should be null in this case.
- removal_condition can be set for effects that need specific actions (e.g. "Find the Witch of the Moor to break this curse"). Can be combined with time_remaining.
- Healing times should be genre/setting-appropriate. Consult the HEALING CONTEXT above.
- TYPE FIELD: Every status effect has a type — "condition" (default) or "threat".
  - "condition": Physical/mental states — injuries, poisons, buffs, curses, tasks in progress.
  - "threat": Approaching dangers, looming consequences, ticking time-bombs. Examples: pursuing enemies, structural collapse, incoming storms, spreading fires, political deadlines.
  - If you omit type, it defaults to "condition".
- All effects persist when their timer reaches zero until you explicitly remove them. The client never auto-removes effects.
- If nothing changed about status effects this turn, omit status_effect_updates entirely.

HARD MODE RULES (MANDATORY):
- choice_metadata MUST include outcome, consequence, and narration_directive for every choice
- Maintain safe_choice_ratio: approximately 0.35 of choices should be advance_safe
- Lethal status effects are allowed but MUST be foreshadowed. Use add with lethal: true. There should have been clues in earlier passages.
- Lethal effects must give the player at least one turn to address them (set time_remaining in the add to allow at least one more scene).
- Full severity range (0.0-1.0). Injuries are serious and heal realistically.
- Threat-type effects should have short time windows before triggering.
- NPCs have low forgiveness. Burning a relationship has lasting consequences.
- Include at least one advance_risky or severe_penalty outcome per set of choices.
- game_over outcomes are available — use for genre-appropriate failures (death, permanent loss, catastrophic failure).
- You may use advances_act and conclusion outcomes when pacing conditions are met.

The Writer produced the following passage and choices this turn.

PASSAGE:
The Alliance reinforcements settle into attack formation around Serenity, their weapons creating a deadly constellation of firepower that eliminates any hope of escape. Chen's final gambit has played out exactly as she planned, with overwhelming force now backing her words.

"Smart man, Reynolds. Here's my offer," Chen's voice cuts through the bridge speakers with cold precision. "You surrender yourself to Alliance custody for questioning about Browncoat networks and assets. In exchange, I allow the independent ships to leave this system unharmed. They'll carry word of your noble sacrifice across the rim - the great Malcolm Reynolds, dying to protect innocent lives."

Zoe's jaw tightens beside you, her hand hovering near her weapon. Jayne shifts nervously, calculating odds that clearly don't favor survival. Kaylee's face has gone pale, her usual optimism crushed under the weight of Chen's cruel mathematics.

"Of course," Chen continues with mock sympathy, "Project Mindbridge continues. Your crew becomes test subjects, and we expand operations to other rim worlds. But those ships out there? They live to spread the legend of your martyrdom. It's quite poetic, really."

The bridge falls silent except for the hum of Alliance targeting systems. Through the viewscreen, you can see the independent ships holding position, their crews waiting for your decision. Chen has engineered the perfect trap - sacrifice yourself to save strangers while dooming your family, or fight and watch everyone die.

CHOICES PRESENTED TO PLAYER:
A: Accept Chen's terms and surrender yourself to save the independent ships
B: Reject the deal and prepare for a final desperate fight
C: Propose a counter-offer that might save everyone
D: Stall for time while looking for another option

Based on the passage and the current game state, determine:
1. State updates — what changed mechanically as a result of this passage (status effects, inventory, time_elapsed)
2. Choice metadata — classify each choice and predetermine its outcome for the next turn

data.passage:
You step forward, hands raised, and the bridge falls silent except for the hum of Alliance ships positioning themselves around Serenity. "I accept," you say, your voice carrying across the comm to Chen and every ship in the system. "But know this - the 'verse is watching now."

Zoe's hand moves toward her weapon, but you catch her eye and shake your head. "Stand down, Zoe. That's an order." The pain in her expression cuts deeper than any Alliance blade, but she nods once, sharp and military.

"Captain," Kaylee whispers, tears streaming down her face. "We can't just let you—"

"You can and you will," you interrupt gently. "Take care of Serenity. Take care of each other." You turn to Jayne, who's gripping his gun so tight his knuckles are white. "And Jayne? Thanks for staying."

Chen's voice crackles through the comm, triumphant but strained. "Alliance transports are en route to collect you, Reynolds. The independent ships have sixty seconds to clear the system before I change my mind about their safety."

Through the viewports, you watch the ragtag fleet of rim runners and mining ships begin their retreat, carrying with them the evidence of Project Mindbridge and the story of what happened here. Some battles you win with guns, others with sacrifice.

As the Alliance transport docks with Serenity, you take one last look around the bridge - at Zoe standing at attention despite her tears, at Kaylee clutching her wrench like a lifeline, at Jayne pretending he's not affected. This crew, this family, will survive. They'll keep flying, keep fighting, and keep the truth alive.

The airlock hisses open, and Alliance soldiers in full combat gear step aboard. You walk toward them without resistance, chin up, shoulders back - every inch the Independent soldier who once stood against impossible odds at Serenity Valley.

"You can't stop the signal," you call back to your crew as the soldiers escort you away. "Remember that."

Serenity's engines fire as you're transferred to the Alliance cruiser, carrying your family toward freedom and whatever comes next. In the black of space, the ship grows smaller until it's just another star, but you know it'll keep flying. That's enough. That has to be enough.
